content.full_text:
\! 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lloyd Von Haden 
2089 Foothill 
Vista, California 92084 
Dear Mr. Von Haden: 
September 16, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-87-2ll 
You have written asking whether members of the City of 
Vista's "Citizens Advisory Committee on Redevelopment" are 
subject to the financial disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act").Y Since you are seeking advice 
concerning another person's duties, we consider your letter to 
be a request for informal assistance and can provide you only 
with general guidance.~ 
In our recent telephone conversation, you indicated that 
the group which you refer to in your letter is a redevelopment 
project area committee formed pursuant to section 33385 of the 
Health and Safety Code. 
The Commission has recently issued an 
opinion in In re Rotman, 10 FPPC Ops. 1, No. 86-001 which 
concludes that such committees are subject to the Act's 
financial disclosure and disqualification provisions. 
I have 
enclosed a copy of that opinion for your information. 
I trust this responds to your request. 
If you have any 
further questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
BY:?i::~~~~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 
!I Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
l/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with 
the Immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice 
(Section 83114; Regulation l8329(c) (3).) 
DMG:JGM: jaj 
Enclosure 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804.0814 
Ladies and gentlemen 
July 29, 1987 
AUG 3 e ~y .H1 'B7 
I think I phoned your office July 7 to ask whether the 
individual members of the City of Vista's "Citizens Advisory 
Committee on Redevelopment" had to submit financial disclosure 
statements. 
Your office then sent me material postmarked 
July 7 saying that it appeared the Vista C~C did not have to disclose. 
But acoording to your regulation 18700 tta board or commission 
possases decision-making authority whenever: 
(C) 
It makes substantive recommendations 
which are. and over an extended per10d of time 
have been, regularly approved without sign1ficant 
amendment or modification by another public 
official or governmental agency." 
This committee's recommendations certainly fit that description. 
The oommittee has met regularr since April 1986. and its final draft 
was adopted recently by the Agency and Council without change. 
None of the project area oontains "a substantial number of low 
and moderate-income families (which) are to be displaced, ,t and 
none of the committee members livesthere (33385.5.) 
MY sense of justice tells me that it is wrong for a significant 
peroentage of this committee's members who own or rent business 
property in the project to make deoisions affecting their own 
or rented property without full disclosure. 
There exists too much material concerning this committee's 
activities to submit in this letter. 
I hope your commission can 
look further into this matter. 
Lloyd v 
2089 Foo hill 
Vista, California 92084 
( 619) 724 .. 5601 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lloyd von Haden 
2089 Foothill 
Vista, CA 92084 
Dear Mr. von Haden: 
August 4, 1987 
Re: 
87-211 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on August 3, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the 
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:jaj 
Very truly yours, 
0,-o-,- )h:d-~-r\6TI-, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804#0807 • (916) 322#5660

sections.question: You have written asking whether members of the City of Vista's "Citizens Advisory Committee on Redevelopment" are subject to the financial disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").
sections.conclusion: The Commission has recently issued an opinion in In re Rotman, 10 FPPC Ops. 1, No. 86-001 which concludes that such committees are subject to the Act's financial disclosure and disqualification provisions.
sections.question_synthetic: Are members of the City of Vista's Citizens Advisory Committee on Redevelopment subject to financial disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, members of redevelopment project area committees formed pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 33385 are subject to the Political Reform Act's financial disclosure and disqualification provisions, as established in the Commission's opinion in In re Rotman.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes multiple pages: incoming request letter (July 29, 1987), acknowledgment letter (August 4, 1987), and FPPC response (September 16, 1987). The primary advice letter response is the September 16 letter from Diane M. Griffiths. Requestor provided informal assistance only, without full immunity. Document references enclosed Rotman opinion but opinion text not included in provided excerpt.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have written asking whether members of the City of Vista's "Citizens Advisory Committee on Redevelopment" are subject to the financial disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").

CONCLUSION: The Commission has recently issued an opinion in In re Rotman, 10 FPPC Ops. 1, No. 86-001 which concludes that such committees are subject to the Act's financial disclosure and disqualification provisions.

embedding.summary: FPPC responded to Lloyd Von Haden's inquiry regarding whether members of Vista's Citizens Advisory Committee on Redevelopment must comply with financial disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act. The Commission directed him to its recent Rotman opinion, which concluded that such committees are subject to the Act's disclosure and disqualification provisions.
